proved: Research inspection returns one monotonic state-vector revision and rejects a result if the Yjs document changes during asynchronous validation.
Scenario creation requires exact research revision and project identity; a stale revision creates zero scenario records.
A valid call creates a bounded scenario and a second guarded call can create a branch only when the parent exists.
Guarded add-turn and revise-turn operations chain returned revisions, retain earlier attributed turn bodies, and reject stale mutations without changing history.
Guarded voting chains support, re-vote, and withdrawal events; aggregate projection updates while history is retained, and a stale revision adds no event.

Annotation automation chains create, edit, resolve, and reopen under both the current research revision and exact annotation event; stale project/lifecycle calls add no event and bodies are omitted from readback.
Rust advertises and routes scenario creation, turn add/revise, voting, and annotation lifecycle as the fifteenth through twenty-first semantic MCP tools; compiled stdio discovery requires all seven.

The packaged live harness chains unique scenario creation, turn add/revision, support/re-vote, annotation create/edit/resolve/reopen, bounded metadata readback, and stale rejection, but this run did not mutate the user profile.
notProved: The installed packaged GUI completed the updated live mutation harness.
Model-driven scenario generation, gallery UI, authenticated voting/annotation identity, moderation, or evaluation through MCP.
Authenticated participant identity, Drive/WebSocket project transport, or multi-install concurrency.
